mnemonics.title: SMART Contract Characteristics
mnemonics.content: S - Self-executing, M - Mandatory terms, A - Automated enforcement, R - Recorded on blockchain, T - Trustless execution

mnemonics.title: Solidity Data Locations
mnemonics.content: S - Storage (persistent), M - Memory (temporary), C - Calldata (immutable, temporary) - Remember: 'SMaC' for Storage memory calldata

mnemonics.title: Reentrancy Prevention (CEI Pattern)
mnemonics.content: C - Checks first, E - Effects (state changes) second, I - Interactions (external calls) last

acronyms.usage: An organization governed by smart contracts where decisions are made by member votes on proposals.
The runtime environment that executes smart contract bytecode on the Ethereum network.
An application that runs on a decentralized network, often using smart contracts as its backend logic.
Financial services (lending, borrowing, trading) built using smart contracts, removing traditional intermediaries.
A technical document defining standards for Ethereum tokens and smart contract implementations.
A fundraising method using smart contracts where investors receive tokens in exchange for cryptocurrency.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart: User triggers transaction → Smart contract receives → Validates conditions (require) → Executes logic → Updates state → Emits events → Transaction confirmed on blockchain. Show how gas flows opposite to execution.

Think of gas as 'fuel for a car journey'. Each operation (ADD, SSTORE, CALL) costs specific gas units. Complex operations (storage write) cost more. Total gas = sum of all operation costs. If gas runs out mid-journey, you return to starting point (revert).

Draw two boxes representing Victim Contract and Attacker Contract. Show: 1) Attacker calls withdraw(), 2) Contract sends ETH, 3) Attacker fallback calls withdraw() AGAIN before balance updates, 4) Loop continues until contract empty. Add a 'Do Not Enter' sign between steps 2 and 3 representing the missing state update.

analogies.concept: Smart Contract as a Vending Machine
analogies.analogy: Like a vending machine: Insert money (trigger) → Select item (condition) → Machine automatically dispenses (action) → No middleman needed. If you don't pay or selection is empty, nothing happens (revert).

Gas is like fuel for your car journey through the EVM. Different roads (operations) cost different fuel. Longer trips (complex contracts) need more fuel. If you run out mid-journey, you stop and pay for the distance traveled (gas consumed, no refund).

Smart contracts are in a 'kitchen' (blockchain) that can't see outside. Oracles are like waiters who bring 'orders' (external data like prices, weather) from customers (real world) to the kitchen to prepare meals (execute contracts).
